title: Entergy New Orleans hosts annual Energize NOLA Community Fair and Backpack Giveaway
summary: More than 650 community members gathered at the Joe Brown Park Recreation Center for Entergy New Orleans’ annual Energize NOLA Community Fair and Backpack Giveaway, an event designed to help local families prepare for the upcoming school year while connecting them with valuable community resources and energy-saving programs.

title: IAEA scientists help rainbow trout farmers in Latin America
summary: The trout population in the western hemisphere is getting stronger through the application of nuclear science. The International Atomic Energy Agency, as part of a technical cooperation project, is equipping laboratories and training specialists in nine countries to better deal with the infectious pancreatic necrosis virus (IPNV) in rainbow trout.

title: Serbian officials lay out nuclear energy timeline
summary: p.p1 {margin: 0.0px 0.0px 8.0px 0.0px; font: 12.0px Aptos; color: #000000} span.s1 {text-decoration: underline; color: #386573} Serbia reached a significant milestone in 2024 when its National Assembly overturned a moratorium on nuclear power plants that had been in place for 35 years, a ban imposed when the country was part of the former Yugoslavia. Fast forward to 2026 and the European country’s nuclear energy ambitions have become clearer. On July 13, Serbia’s Ministry of Mining and Energy held a four-day workshop outlining the country’s plans to kickstart a nuclear program.

title: Entergy’s Louisiana utilities urge customers to prepare as tropical weather approaches Louisiana
summary: The companies encourage customers to take the time now to get personal storm plans in place and make a kit with basic emergency supplies, including any medical needs that require electricity.

title: LLNL and Pacific Fusion achieve 3,000-shot milestone with Sirius pulsed-power prototype
summary: p.p1 {margin: 0.0px 0.0px 8.0px 0.0px; font: 10.0px Verdana; color: #000000} span.s1 {text-decoration: underline; color: #0000ff} span.s2 {font: 12.0px 'Times New Roman'} Researchers from Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory and Pacific Fusion have surpassed 3,000 “shots” with Sirius, a four-stage prototype impedance-matched Marx generator (IMG) of the type that can provide pulsed power to an inertial confinement fusion machine. The team characterized this achievement as a “key milestone in the development of high-gain fusion and a practical example of government-industry partnership at work.”

title: Vamsi Chadalavada engages with groups across region in first 6 months leading ISO-NE
summary: The CEO participated in forums exploring cold weather operations, large loads, energy affordability, and resource adequacy. The post Vamsi Chadalavada engages with groups across region in first 6 months leading ISO-NE appeared first on ISO Newswire.
